dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 28, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**RAYS SHUT DOWN GUARDIANS IN 1-0 THRILLER**

In a low-scoring game, the Tampa Bay Rays edged the Cleveland Guardians 1-0 behind a strong outing from Tanner Bibee, who allowed just one earned run in 5.0 IP. Nick Martinez earned the win for the Rays, while Cole Sulser closed out the game for the save. On the other side, Jonathan Aranda drove in the game's only run with a single to right field, scoring Taylor Walls.

**ORIOLES BASH ASTROS 5-3**

The Baltimore Orioles bounced back from an early deficit to take down the Houston Astros 5-3. Brice Matthews led the charge with a 3-hit, 2-RBI performance, including a solo homer. Shane Baz earned the win for the Orioles, while Ryan Helsley closed out the game for the save.

**REDS RUMBLE TO 7-2 WIN OVER ROCKIES**

The Cincinnati Reds dominated the Colorado Rockies 7-2, behind a strong outing from Chase Burns, who struck out 9 in 6.0 IP. Elly De La Cruz led the Reds' offense with a 3-hit, 4-RBI performance, including a solo homer.

**CARDINALS SWEEP PIRATES 11-7**

The St. Louis Cardinals took down the Pittsburgh Pirates 11-7, behind a strong team effort. Ryan O'Hearn led the Cardinals' offense with a 2-hit, 3-RBI performance, including a solo homer. Kyle Leahy earned the win for the Cardinals, while Braxton Ashcraft took the loss.

**PHILLIES SHUT DOWN GIANTS 7-0**

The Philadelphia Phillies dominated the San Francisco Giants 7-0, behind a strong outing from Jesús Luzardo, who allowed just 2 hits in 7.0 IP. Trea Turner led the Phillies' offense with a 4-hit performance.

**BLUE JAYS TAKE DOWN RED SOX 3-0**

The Toronto Blue Jays shut down the Boston Red Sox 3-0, behind a strong team effort. Vladimir Guerrero Jr. led the Blue Jays' offense with a single to center field, scoring Myles Straw.

**METs TAKE DOWN NATIONALS 8-0**

The New York Mets dominated the Washington Nationals 8-0, behind a strong outing from Clay Holmes, who allowed just 3 hits in 6.0 IP. Juan Soto led the Mets' offense with a 2-hit performance, including a solo homer.

**BRAVES TAKE DOWN TIGERS 5-2**

The Atlanta Braves took down the Detroit Tigers 5-2, behind a strong outing from Martín Pérez, who allowed just 2 hits in 5.0 IP. Wenceel Pérez led the Braves' offense with a 1-hit, 2-RBI performance, including a solo homer.

**MARINERS DOMINATE TWINS 7-1**

The Seattle Mariners dominated the Minnesota Twins 7-1, behind a strong outing from Joe Ryan, who allowed just 2 earned runs in 6.0 IP. Josh Naylor led the Mariners' offense with a 3-hit, 4-RBI performance, including a solo homer.

**WHITE SOX TAKE DOWN ANGELS 5-2**

The Chicago White Sox took down the Los Angeles Angels 5-2, behind a strong outing from Davis Martin, who allowed just 1 earned run in 5.2 IP. Drew Romo led the White Sox' offense with a 2-hit, 3-RBI performance, including a pair of solo homers.

**BREWERS DOMINATE DIAMONDBACKS 13-2**

The Milwaukee Brewers dominated the Arizona Diamondbacks 13-2, behind a strong team effort. Sal Frelick led the Brewers' offense with a 2-hit, 1-RBI performance, including a solo homer.

**YANKEES TAKE DOWN RANGERS 3-2**

The New York Yankees took down the Texas Rangers 3-2, behind a strong outing from Cam Schlittler, who allowed just 2 earned runs in 6.0 IP. Aaron Judge led the Yankees' offense with a 2-hit, 1-RBI performance, including a solo homer.

**ROYALS TAKE DOWN ATHLETICS 4-1**

The Kansas City Royals took down the Athletics 4-1, behind a strong outing from Kris Bubic, who allowed just 1 earned run in 5.0 IP. Bobby Witt Jr. led the Royals' offense with a 1-hit, 3-RBI performance, including a solo homer.

**CUBS TAKE DOWN PADRES 8-3**

The Chicago Cubs took down the San Diego Padres 8-3, behind a strong outing from Edward Cabrera, who allowed just 3 earned runs in 5.2 IP. Pete Crow-Armstrong led the Cubs' offense with a 1-hit, 3-RBI performance, including a solo homer.

**MARLINS TAKE DOWN DODGERS 2-1**

The Miami Marlins took down the Los Angeles Dodgers 2-1, behind a strong outing from Shohei Ohtani, who allowed just 1 earned run in 6.0 IP. Will Smith led the Marlins' offense with a 3-hit performance.
